sentences.en: A paragraph should advance one controlling idea; each sentence ought to pull in the same direction, like oars that would otherwise splash uselessly if each rower aimed at a different shore.
sentences.zh: 一段文字应当只推进一个中心意思；各句须朝同一方向发力，好比众人划船若各指一岸，桨击便只剩水花、不见前行。

sentences.en: When a sentence wanders without handing the reader a clear link, the whole passage begins to feel like a polite pile of observations rather than an argument you can walk across.
sentences.zh: 若某句岔开却未交接到读者手中，整段便会沦为彬彬有礼的观察堆砌，难成一条走得的论证之路。
